events.title: Visual Language Library Pop-Up
events.venue: Library, W. E. B. Du Bois
events.description: As part of the Festival of Languages and Dialect, join us for a special pop-up event at the Libraries, where you can make and display zines, check out the Libraries' graphic novels collection, and join a Tatreez circle to learn traditional Palestinian embroidery.

events.title: Sefardi Herbalism: An Introduction to Earth, Water and Plant-Based Folk Traditions
events.description: Naomi Spector is a writer, educator, and herbalist. She will speak about her new book, Sefardi Herbalism: An Introduction to Earth, Water and Plant-Based Folk Traditions of the Sefardim; she also wrote The Jewish Book of Flowers. Naomi is a co-founder of bibi + ni and teaches courses on Jewish kitchen herbalism, amulets, end-of-life herbalism and more. She holds a Masters of Arts in Education from Tufts University. Visit The Herb Library after the talk (one flight up). *Sefardim are Jews from Iberia (Spain and Portugal) and their descendants.

events.title: Festival of Languages and Dialects
events.description: Come to the Festival of Languages and Dialects, a mini-convention and carnival that celebrates our rainbow of languages. We are excited to feature live music, dance, puppetry, talks, workshops, and a parade. Most of all, we are excited for members of different language communities to meet and learn how to support each other. Saturday & Sunday, April 11 & 12 Saturday: 9:00am-4:00pm Sunday: 9:00am-2:45pm Events are primarily in Thompson Hall, but some activities are happening in Machmer Hall. Saturday at 4:00pm we all parade to Amherst Town Common!

events.title: Workshop: Emotionally Support Yourself
events.description: Prepare for moments of anxiety or stress with strategies and resources to calm body and mind. April is Sexual Assault Awareness Month (SAAM).

events.title: ASK THE ATTORNEY SERIES
events.description: Ask the Attorney DROP-IN Hour for INTERNATIONAL STUDENTS Do you have immigration related questions? Join us for FREE, confidential legal consulation with SLSO Attorney Ben Farrell. Tuesday, February 10, 2026 2:00 to 3:00 PMTuesday, March 10, 2026 2:00 to 3:00 PMTuesday, March 31, 2026 2:00 to 3:00 PMTuesday, April 14, 2026 2:00 to 3:00 PMTuesday, April 28, 2026 2:00 to 3:00 PM

events.title: Tay Gavin Erickson Lecture Series - Dr. David Weinshenker: Locus Coeruleus Pathology in Alzheimer’s Disease
events.description: Tay Gavin Erickson Lecture Series Dr. David Weinshenker; Emory University Mechanisms and Consequences of Locus Coeruleus Pathology in Alzheimer’s Disease Register Here! The locus coeruleus (LC), the main source of norepinephrine (NE) in the central nervous system, is one of the first brain regions to accumulate pathology in the earliest stages of Alzheimer’s disease and undergoes catastrophic degeneration as disease progresses. Early LC-NE dysfunction is coincident with neuropsychiatric symptoms, while later LC degeneration predicts and exacerbates cognitive impairment. This talk will present

events.title: 2026 Freeman Lecture in Linguistics, Featuring Robert Frank (Yale University)
events.description: It is our great pleasure to welcome to campus Professor Robert Frank of Yale University, who will be presenting this year’s Freeman Lecture in Linguistics on April 14th. The Freeman Lecture is held annually at UMass, and is named in honor of the founding head of our Linguistics Department, Donald C. Freeman, and his wife Margaret Freeman. These talks are designed for a broad, cross-disciplinary audience of students and faculty across campus. Professor Frank is a renowned expert in both computational linguistics and syntax. A foundational member of Yale University’s Computational Linguistics at

events.title: Be Seriously Scared! A Program on Nuclear Weapons
events.description: The Ellsberg Initiative for Peace and Democracy will host “Be Seriously Scared!”, an interactive program that combines performance art, historical storytelling, and documentary film to confront the ongoing dangers of nuclear weapons and explore pathways toward a world free of nuclear war.

events.title: Book Party with Professors Shabazz and Smethurst
events.description: Please join us for a Book Party to celebrate the publication of books by Professor Amilcar Shabazz and Professor James Smethurst. Books will be available for purchase. Light refreshments will be provided!

events.title: Understanding Autism: Evidence, Myth, and Realities
events.description: This event is part of the UMass Springfield Center Faculty Lecture Series. Dinner is provided for attendees and RSVP is requested. Moderated by Dr. Megan Gross, Speech-language pathologist and Associate Professor of Speech, Language, and Hearing Sciences at the University of Massachusetts Amherst. This panel will bring together faculty with varied expertise, including special guest Dr. Helen Tager-Flusberg, Professor Emerita and Director of the Center for Autism Research Excellence at Boston University. The panel will address common questions prompted by current discussions about autism in the

events.title: Defining and Identifying Islamophobia workshop for undergraduates
events.description: As part of our Multifaith UMass initiative, undergraduate students are invited to two introductory workshops on antisemitism and Islamophobia. Registration is encouraged, but not required, to attend the sessions. Defining and Identifying Islamophobia April 14, 2026, 6:00 - 7:30 pm, Goodell Hall 329 Defining and Identifying Antisemitism April 21, 2026, 6:00 - 7:30 pm, Goodell Hall 329 The sessions are facilitated by Brad Seligmann, Director of the Office of Religious and Spiritual Life.

events.title: The Translated Book Club: Neighbours
events.description: The Translated Book Club promotes the reading of literature translated into English and recognizes and supports translators and their publishers. Each meeting is hosted at UMass Downtown by a UMass Amherst faculty member or graduate student and designed for readers of diverse backgrounds with no specific language requirement other than English. Sponsored by the Gentzler Translation Center, the club is free and open to community members from high school students to retirees, with a maximum of 15 readers per meeting. Advanced sign-up is required at https://www.umass.edu/downtown/news-events. The

events.title: Workshop: Support your People
events.description: Learn about what you might say and do if someone shares that they've experienced violence. Tues 4/14 6pm-7pm | Goodell 302 | Cosponsored by CMASS and Sigma Psi Zeta Mon 4/27 2:30-3:30pm | New Africa House Room 213| April is Sexual Assault Awareness Month (SAAM).

events.title: BPQC Meeting: Responding to Prejudice
events.description: The Bi/Pan/Queer Collective (BPQC), a social group for bi, pan, queer, and sexually fluid students, will be discussing responses to prejudice at its 4/14 meeting. Open to all students.

events.title: Libraries' Page to Screen Club
events.description: Drop in to the Libraries' multimedia book club, where we read a book and then watch the screen adaptation! We meet for several weeks to talk about the book, then watch the screen adaptation together and compare. All sessions are 7-8:30 p.m. except for screenings, which are 7-9:30 p.m. The breakdown for what pages/sections to read will be determined at a later planning session. Little Women, Louisa May Alcott February 3 February 10 February 17 February 24 March 3 - Screening Stories of Your Life and Others, Ted Chiang March 10 March 17 - No meeting; spring vacation March 24 - Screening Jurassic

events.title: Jazz Lab Ensemble
events.description: Join us for this free big band jazz concert in Bezanson; Jazz Lab is directed by Aidan Higgins and Fletcher Clark.

events.title: Deep Dive: MS in CS Info Session
events.description: Explore your path to an MS in Computer Science. Join this virtual information session to learn about our on-campus, online, and hybrid program options. We’ll walk you through the application process—from requirements to key materials like your statement of purpose and letters of recommendation—so you know exactly what to expect. Leave with a clear understanding of how to apply and what you need to do next. Register now to save your spot.

events.title: Paws: Therapy Dogs
events.venue: Campus Center Auditorium, W.E.B. Du Bois Library & Science and Engineering Library
events.description: Students have the opportunity to take a "Paws" to hangout with some amazing therapy dogs! Come visit some therapy dogs at the Paws event to connect and relax. There will also be opportunities to connect with Peer Health Educators (PHEs) and each other to engage in calming activities. You work hard; give yourself a well-deserved break. Location : Campus Center Auditorium, W.E.B. Du Bois Library & Science and Engineering Library Date: 3/2/2026 10:30am to 12:30pm

events.title: Bee Hotel Refresh Day
events.description: The UMass Bee Campus Committee invites you to join us for the second annual bee hotel refresh! What is a bee hotel? Do they really benefit native bees? Why is it important to clean them? Come help us build new bee nesting structures and find out the answers to these questions and more! RSVP HERE: https://forms.gle/DtiVaygHCV6ocpgW8 Brought to you by the UMass Bee Campus Committee, and the UMass Grounds, Events, Facilities, and Campus Services Department, in collaboration with the Franklin Permaculture Garden, the Beekeeping Club, the Fernald Club, the MassPirg “Save the Bees” campaign, and the

events.title: Voices of Data Science Conference
events.description: With Voices of Data Science, our mission is to provide a platform to amplify the voices of data scientists and foster a robust and inclusive community of individuals who share a vision for utilizing data science ethically. Voices of Data Science (VDS) is an annual data science conference hosted at UMass Amherst and open to undergraduate and graduate students interested in pursuing data science and related fields as a career.
